name: OpenAI Whisper (Local ASR / Speech-to-Text)

auth.notes: Local inference; no API key needed (unlike OpenAI API Whisper)

performance.notes: GPU: real-time or faster; CPU: 2-5x slower than audio duration for large model

agent_readiness.notes: OpenAI's open source speech recognition model. import whisper. model = whisper.load_model('base'). result = model.transcribe('audio.mp3'). print(result['text']). Models: tiny, base, small, medium, large, large-v2, large-v3. result['segments']: [{start, end, text}]. result['language']. whisper.detect_language(model, mel). Multilingual: 99 languages. CLI: whisper audio.mp3 --model large --language Japanese. Word timestamps: model.transcribe('audio.mp3', word_timestamps=True). faster-whisper uses CTranslate2 for 4-8x speedup. PyTorch required. ~1.5GB VRAM for large. Diarization via pyannote-audio integration. Local — no data sent to OpenAI.

gotchas: Original whisper is slow — use faster-whisper (4-8x faster) or whisperX
Large model ~3GB download on first use
No streaming — full audio file only
Hallucinations on silence or noisy audio
Word timestamps less accurate than speaker diarization tools
best_when: Local/private speech-to-text without sending data to OpenAI API
When faster-whisper's CTranslate2 speed is acceptable
avoid_when: Production scale without GPU (CPU is too slow)
When OpenAI's hosted Whisper API latency is acceptable
summary: OpenAI Whisper transcribes speech locally. model = whisper.load_model('base'|'small'|'medium'|'large'); result = model.transcribe('audio.mp3'); result['text'], result['segments']. 99 languages. Word timestamps. MIT by OpenAI. PyTorch required. Use faster-whisper for 4-8x speedup. No streaming. Large model ~3GB. Best for private batch transcription. OpenAI API Whisper for cloud with latency budget.
